name: 3D Web Configurator Specification
description: Complete blueprint for recreating this immersive Three.js web configurator from scratch. Share this file alongside the GLB, HDR, and configurator JSON to reproduce the full experience.

model.note_on_size: If the GLB exceeds 100 MB, split it into two chunks for GitHub Pages hosting. The browser fetches both in parallel and merges the ArrayBuffers before parsing.

split_strategy.split_command: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const b=fs.readFileSync('model.glb'); const m=Math.ceil(b.length/2); fs.writeFileSync('model.part1.glb',b.slice(0,m)); fs.writeFileSync('model.part2.glb',b.slice(m));"
split_strategy.browser_merge: fetch both, new Uint8Array(b1 + b2), pass .buffer to GLTFLoader.parse()

environment.usage: scene.environment only (not scene.background) — keeps the dark UI aesthetic
environment.fallback: If HDR fails to load, activate ambient + directional lights automatically

config.description: Exported from Blender addon. Defines colour variant hex codes + target mesh names, and geometry variant mesh visibility groups.

blender_export_workflow.addon: Custom Blender configurator addon v2.0.0 (compatible with Blender 5.x)
what_it_exports: GLB with all variant meshes included (both visible and hidden)
configurator_bike.json listing each variant group, hex colours, mesh names, and material slot indices

glb_export_settings.mesh_naming: Objects must keep their Blender names — the configurator matches by mesh.name

COLOR.purpose: Recolour specific meshes by setting material slot 0 to a new hex colour

GEO.purpose: Show/hide mutually exclusive sets of meshes (e.g. handlebar style A vs B)

tech_stack.renderer: Three.js r167 (ESM via import map — zero bundler, zero build step)

loaders.DRACOLoader: three/addons/loaders/DRACOLoader.js — decoder served from jsDelivr CDN

camera.initial_position: computed from model bounding box after load

model_loading.step_1_fetch: Fetch part1 and part2 in parallel with Promise.all
model_loading.step_2_merge: Concatenate Uint8Arrays into a single ArrayBuffer

step_4_centre.position: shift so model bottom sits at Y=0, centred on X/Z

how_it_works: 1. After model load, collect all mesh names referenced in any colour variant's target_objects.
2. For each such mesh, clone its material at mat_slot index (default 0) so changes are independent per mesh.
3. On colour selection, animate mat.color from current → new hex using cubic ease-in-out over ~0.35 s.

animation.method: THREE.Color.lerpColors per frame in render loop

colour_variant_system.important: Clone materials ONCE at load time — never on every colour change

how_it_works: 1. Collect all object names from ALL geo variant groups into a Set.
2. On load, hide every object in that Set (mesh.visible = false).
3. On variant selection, hide the full Set again, then show only the selected variant's objects.
geometry_variant_system.default_variant: index 1 (Variant 2 — the larger, more complete set)

camera_presets.how_it_works: Lerp camera.position and controls.target each frame toward target values (0.048 blend factor).

default.label: 3/4 View
default.position: diagLen*0.7 on X and Z, mid height

top.label: Top
top.position: directly above, slight Z offset to avoid gimbal lock

camera_presets.all_targets: all presets look at the model's bounding box centre

layout.bottom_panel: fixed centred panel, max-width 740px, two sections side by side
layout.section_1: Colour Variant — circular swatches with ring selection indicator

colour_swatch.selected_indicator: 2px white ring via border on parent .swatch-ring element
colour_swatch.glow: box-shadow on .swatch-dot matching the hex colour

ui_design.vignette: radial-gradient overlay (transparent centre → black edges) for depth
ui_design.drag_hint: fades out after 5 seconds of inactivity

hosting.recommended: GitHub Pages (free, static, no server needed)
hosting.deployment: GitHub Actions workflow — pushes to Pages on every commit to main

large_file_strategy.problem: GitHub Pages cannot serve Git LFS files (serves pointer text instead). GitHub Releases/LFS CDN blocks browser CORS.
large_file_strategy.solution: Split GLB into two chunks each under 100 MB. Push as regular git files. Browser merges at runtime.

hosting.local_dev: npx serve . (any static server) — required because browsers block file:// fetches

file_structure.index.html: Complete self-contained configurator (HTML + CSS + JS, no build step)
file_structure.configurator_bike.json: Variant definitions exported from Blender addon
file_structure.<model>.part1.glb: First half of the GLB binary (if model > 100 MB)
file_structure.<model>.part2.glb: Second half of the GLB binary
file_structure.<model>.glb: Original full GLB (keep locally, not pushed if > 100 MB)
file_structure.<environment>.hdr: HDR environment map for IBL lighting
file_structure..gitignore: Excludes node_modules, OS files, editor files

reproduction_steps.summary: Given a GLB + HDR + configurator_bike.json + this spec, reproduce the configurator in ~10 minutes.
steps: 1. Place all files in a folder.
2. If GLB > 100 MB: run the split_command in assets.model.split_strategy to create .part1 and .part2 files.
3. Copy the index.html template (from this repo or recreate from this spec).
4. In index.html, update ASSETS paths if filenames differ from defaults.
5. Update colour/variant display names in COLOR_NAMES / VARIANT_NAMES arrays if desired.
6. Run 'npx serve .' and open http://localhost:3000 to test locally.
7. Push to GitHub, enable Pages (Settings → Pages → GitHub Actions source), done.
